title: Entity Relationships
description: Understand how entities relate to each other in our data model.
question: What happens to a user's tasks when we delete that user from the database, based on our data model design?
options: The tasks remain in the database with a null owner
The deletion fails because tasks still reference the user
The tasks are also deleted (cascade delete)
The tasks are automatically assigned to an admin user
explanation: In a typical Task Manager data model, the User-to-Task relationship uses CascadeType.ALL, meaning when a user is deleted, all their tasks are automatically deleted as well (cascade delete). This ensures referential integrity without leaving orphaned task records in the database.
